Job Description: K-group Leader

Opportunity: K-Group Leader

Summary:

Kaleo's K-Group ministry is the backbone of how our community is structured. Our people gather weekly in homes to fellowship, serve, learn and grow spiritually. A K-Group Leader is a single person or married couple willing to gather and shepherd a small group (Koinonia-Group, AKA K-Group) of congregants on a weekly basis.

Time Requirements:

- K-group leaders should plan on leading a once-weekly gathering in a home for 3+/- hours from September-May (K-group seasons are matched to the school year). For summer and December, we only ask that you gather your K-group once a month.

- The K-groups pastor will lead a topical training (typically with food and childcare provided) about 4 times a year (October, January, March, May) that will last about 2-3 hours.

- Twice a semester, the K-group pastor will meet with you (spouses encouraged to join) for a check-in. Normally this is done over lunch or dinner.

- Every August, we ask that our existing and new K-group leaders gather once a week for 3-weeks for about 2-3 hours each week to go through a training &ldquo;boot camp&rdquo; before kicking off a new season. Dinner and childcare provided.

This Position Reports to: K-Groups Director &ndash; Pastor Cody Carpenter

Responsibilities and Authority:

- Gathering your K-group weekly. Communication is key.

- Leading your K-Group by praying, teaching/learning the bible, being on mission, fellowshipping and eating together. Not every one of these elements will happen in a weekly gathering but they should be happening throughout the course of a ministry season.

- K-group leaders do not necessarily need to host the group in their own home, but it can be helpful if they are able to. Hosting can happen at another participant&rsquo;s house or at the church if needed.

- Leaders will need to lead the group and help delegate duties to other members.

- Keep their K-group "Groups" page updated with pertinent information (attendance, vision for the group, communication with the group) and be responsive and warm when a new member requests to join.

Qualifications:

- Have a sincere and mature walk with the Lord.

- Be part of an existing K-group.

- Have your current K-group leader&rsquo;s recommendation to lead.

- Be a Kaleo Church member in good standing.

- Possess a desire to lead and a willingness to submit to the Lord&rsquo;s direction and to church leadership.

- Pass a background check. If there is a flag in your background, it does not necessarily disqualify you. We would just ask to have a conversation about it and take it case-by-case, dependent on the severity, time since offense, and the applicant's maturity.

Ready to Apply?
